title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Program Brief: Redirect Graph
summary: Titles become unstable routes, Unicode collapses into empty slugs, case-only paths collide on deployment, old links vanish after rename, and canonical URLs point at preview origins. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the redirect graph increment in collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Validate old-to-new redirects for loops, chains, conflicts, missing targets, host support, and retention ownership.
Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-redirect-graph — Validate old-to-new redirects for loops, chains, conflicts, missing targets, host support, and retention ownership.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_slugs_permalinks_4(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"

Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-redirect-graph-1u1em2
# Competency: ssg-redirect-graph.
# Case 7090: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_redirect_graph_1u1em2(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1u1em2 = "1u1em2-2me1u1-7090"
    assert evidence_variant_1u1em2
    evidence_axes_1u1em2 = (
        "reader1u1em2",
        "source7090",
        "parse2me1u1",
        "route1u1em27090",
        "artifact70901u1em2",
        "recovery2me1u17090",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1u1em2)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate old-to-new redirects for loops, chains, conflicts, missing targets, host support, and retention ownership.
